note: Pre-generated briefs and outreach drafts for 15 FIT prospects. Triggered by double-click on FIT cards in database.html. Authored via Claude in Cowork against project ICP and JSON data on 2026-05-28.

brief.who: Kelsey Newberry leads brand-level work on eos Body, the category extension eos has been scaling since 2024. eos is a private DTC personal care company, estimated revenue $200-300M. The brand's reputation runs on bold, irreverent creative work: the Britney Spears body lotion campaign, the bigfoot 'Bless Your F***ing Cooch' shaving cream ad that went viral. eos doesn't do safe.
brief.why: Exact ICP target. VP-level title at a DTC challenger in beauty/personal care, well under $500M, fast-moving category. Her Cannes session is literally about amplifying a brand through culture, which is the CYLNDR pitch. She's one of three confirmed eos contacts at Cannes (with President Soyoung Kang and SVP Marketing Carley Caldas). Concentrated company access opportunity.
hooks: Her session covers what to do after a campaign hit. The frame: how do you keep cultural momentum after the Britney moment?
Body extension is the operational challenge right now. She's evaluating partners who can move at challenger brand pace.
The bigfoot ad lineage suggests eos rewards partners who take real creative swings. Lead with conviction, not safety.

drafts.warm: Hi Kelsey, I'll be at Brand Innovators on Wednesday for your session on amplifying eos Body. The Britney lotion campaign was a masterclass in turning a category extension into a cultural moment, and I want to know how you're thinking about what comes next. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. We work fast with challenger brands that take real creative swings. Coffee at the Carlton terrace after your panel?
drafts.direct: Kelsey, saw you're speaking Wed Jun 24 on eos Body. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's in-house creative and production studio. We work with DTC brands that need partners who can move at challenger pace. 15 minutes Wed afternoon? I'll come to you.
drafts.curious: Kelsey, your session caught my eye because the post-Britney question is the one I'd want to be working on right now. The cultural hit landed. The category extension is the harder play. I run growth at CYLNDR, a creative and production studio that partners with DTC brands on exactly that arc. Worth 15 minutes at Cannes?

brief.who: SVP Marketing & Creative at eos Products, leading both brand and creative for the DTC personal care brand. eos is the same private company described in Newberry's profile, est. $200-300M revenue, bold/irreverent creative reputation (Britney lotion, viral bigfoot ad). hooks: FQ Beach session is about turning insight into creative work that lands. The CYLNDR frame: how do you compress that loop for challenger brands?

brief.who: Kory Marchisotto is CMO of e.l.f. Beauty, the DTC challenger cosmetics brand that has become one of the most-discussed marketing stories of the past three years. e.l.f. is publicly traded (~$1.5B revenue), but operates with the urgency and creative fearlessness of a brand a quarter its size. Known for TikTok-first campaigns, value-based positioning, and bold cultural partnerships, e.l.f. has outpunched every legacy beauty brand in earned media per dollar spent.
brief.why: e.l.f. Beauty is a named CYLNDR ICP example brand. Marchisotto owns the entire marketing agenda and is the decision-maker for creative and production partnerships at scale. She is one of five confirmed e.l.f. contacts at Cannes 2026, creating a concentrated company access opportunity across multiple venues. At Cannes, e.l.f. is one of the highest-profile challenger brands in the building.
hooks: e.l.f.'s marketing playbook is the one every DTC beauty challenger is trying to replicate — the question for Marchisotto is what comes after the TikTok-first formula when every brand copies it.
High-volume, fast-cycle creative production is the operational challenge at e.l.f.'s scale. Partners who can match that pace without sacrificing brand voice are rare.
e.l.f. rewards creative conviction over safety, which is legible in every campaign. Lead with a point of view in the first conversation.

brief.who: Laurie Lam is Chief Brand Officer at e.l.f. Beauty, responsible for the brand strategy and creative vision of the DTC cosmetics challenger. She speaks at Brand Innovators Beach Stage on Friday Jun 25 in a fireside chat. e.l.f. is publicly traded (~$1.5B revenue) and known for cultural agility, creator-first campaigns, and irreverent brand voice that consistently outperforms its price-point positioning.
brief.why: e.l.f. Beauty is a named CYLNDR ICP example brand. As CBO, Lam owns the brand's creative identity and is a senior decision-maker for production and creative partnerships. She is the fifth confirmed e.l.f. Beauty executive at Cannes 2026, representing the deepest single-company concentration opportunity in the prospect list. Her Brand Innovators session on Friday creates a clear anchor point for outreach.
hooks: Her Brand Innovators fireside is on the last day of the festival — Friday energy rewards direct, honest conversations over formal pitches.
CBO at a publicly traded DTC brand means she's balancing brand equity with shareholder expectations. Partners who can produce at pace without brand drift are the priority.
e.l.f.'s brand voice is one-of-a-kind in mass beauty — irreverent, values-led, creator-native. Any partner CYLNDR references in this conversation needs to match that bar.

drafts.warm: Hi Laurie, I'll be at Brand Innovators on Friday for your fireside. e.l.f.'s brand voice over the past few years has been genuinely singular in mass beauty, and I'm curious what the CBO's version of 'staying sharp at scale' looks like in practice. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. Coffee before or after?
drafts.direct: Laurie, saw you're at Brand Innovators Friday. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. e.l.f. is the brand we reference most in client conversations. 15 minutes Friday afternoon?
drafts.curious: Laurie, maintaining a brand voice as irreverent and consistent as e.l.f.'s across a public company with this many SKUs is the hard part nobody talks about. What's the operational secret? I run growth at CYLNDR. Worth 15 minutes at Cannes?

brief.who: Patrick O'Keefe is Chief Integrated Marketing Officer at e.l.f. Beauty, speaking at Adweek House on Thursday Jun 25 in a session called 'Redefining the Role of Brands in Entertainment.' e.l.f. Beauty is a publicly traded DTC cosmetics challenger (~$1.5B revenue) known for bold creative, TikTok-first campaigns, and cultural partnership work including the viral Chipotle collaboration and the NFL activation. O'Keefe, as CIMO, is effectively the creative and production decision-maker — the person who approves and directs how e.l.f.'s marketing comes to life.
brief.why: Patrick is the most operationally relevant e.l.f. contact at Cannes for CYLNDR. The Chief Integrated Marketing Officer title is the de facto creative and production buy — he coordinates what gets made, at what scale, with which partners. His session is literally about brands in entertainment, which is CYLNDR-shaped work. Five e.l.f. contacts at Cannes creates a company access concentration that rarely appears in festival prospecting.
hooks: 'Redefining the Role of Brands in Entertainment' is precisely the work CYLNDR does — content that earns attention rather than buying it. Walk in ready to reference recent e.l.f. entertainment-format work.
CIMO title signals integrated production is a priority. e.l.f. produces at pace; the question is which partners keep up without adding friction.
e.l.f.'s brand-entertainment work (NFL, EightPM) shows they're betting on formats that require production depth, not just volume. CYLNDR's Cheil heritage gives access to that depth.

drafts.warm: Hi Patrick, I'll be at Adweek House Thursday for 'Redefining the Role of Brands in Entertainment.' e.l.f.'s EightPM and NFL work are the best recent examples of the brand-entertainment format done right, and I want to hear where that's going next. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. Coffee before your panel?
drafts.direct: Patrick, saw you're speaking Thursday at Adweek House on brands and entertainment. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. We're the production partner behind the kind of integrated work you're describing. 15 minutes Thursday?
drafts.curious: Patrick, the brand-entertainment bet at e.l.f. — EightPM, NFL, the integrated format push — signals a production strategy, not just a media strategy. What makes a production partner actually keep up with that pace? I run growth at CYLNDR. Worth 15 minutes at Adweek House?

brief.who: Amanda Calabrese is Co-Founder and CMO of Sequel, a DTC sustainable period care brand founded in 2019. Sequel makes period underwear and sustainable menstrual products, competing in a category that has seen rapid challenger brand growth alongside brands like Thinx and Saalt. As co-founder with full CMO responsibility, Calabrese owns both brand vision and marketing execution with no layers between her and creative decisions. She speaks at Brand Innovators Beach Stage at Cannes Lions 2026.
brief.why: Co-Founder and CMO at a DTC growth-stage consumer health brand is one of the cleanest ICP matches in the list. Right category (consumer health), right company stage (early growth, private), right decision-making authority (no escalation required). The sustainable period care category is expanding fast and brand differentiation through creative is the primary competitive lever. CYLNDR's challenger brand production model is built for exactly this profile.
hooks: Sustainable period care is in the stage where brand voice separates winners from also-rans — the product parity between Sequel, Thinx, and Saalt is real, so creative is the moat.
Founder-CMO structure means the brand has a singular vision and needs a production partner who can translate it, not interpret it. Lead with listening before pitching.
Growth-stage DTC with a social-first audience means content velocity matters. Partners who produce in days, not weeks, win.

drafts.warm: Hi Amanda, I'll be at Brand Innovators this week. Sequel is in the category where brand is the only real differentiator right now, and the brands that win tend to have production partners who match founder vision rather than impose agency voice. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. Coffee on the Croisette?
drafts.direct: Amanda, I'll be at Brand Innovators. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. We work with DTC growth-stage brands on brand-led production at founder pace. 15 minutes?
drafts.curious: Amanda, in the sustainable period care category right now, brand is the moat — product is basically level. What's the creative bet Sequel is making in the next 12 months to pull away? I run growth at CYLNDR. Worth 15 minutes at Cannes?

brief.who: Joe Staples is VP Creative at Coinbase, the consumer-facing crypto and fintech brand. He speaks at Stagwell Sport Beach on Tuesday Jun 23 at 3:30 PM on 'The Next Billion Fan Sport,' a session connecting crypto, sports audiences, and cultural relevance. Coinbase has invested meaningfully in brand and creative — its Super Bowl QR code ad remains one of the most-discussed in recent years, and the brand has been aggressive in sports sponsorship including the NBA deal. As VP Creative, Staples owns how Coinbase expresses itself visually and experientially.
brief.why: VP Creative at a DTC-adjacent consumer fintech challenger is a direct ICP title match. Staples controls Coinbase's creative production and brand expression decisions. The Sport Beach session on crypto and fan culture signals a brand making big creative bets on cultural adjacency — exactly where CYLNDR operates. His session on June 23 is a clear venue anchor for outreach.
hooks: The 'Next Billion Fan Sport' frame suggests Coinbase is investing in cultural relevance, not just product utility. That requires creative production with real culture instincts — not just executional capacity.
VP Creative at this scale is managing volume and quality simultaneously. Partners who can do both without friction are rare.
Coinbase's Super Bowl moment was bold, cheap to execute, and culturally resonant — which is the CYLNDR pitch for challenger brands. Reference it.

drafts.warm: Hi Joe, I'll be at Sport Beach Tuesday for 'The Next Billion Fan Sport.' The Coinbase Super Bowl QR code moment was exactly the kind of work that proves brand conviction beats production budget — and I want to know where you're taking that next. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. Coffee at Sport Beach after?
drafts.direct: Joe, saw you're speaking Tuesday at Sport Beach. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. We work with consumer fintech and challenger brands on bold creative production. 15 minutes Tuesday afternoon?
drafts.curious: Joe, the 'next billion fan sport' framing for crypto is one of the more interesting cultural bets I've seen from a financial brand. What does brand creative look like when you're trying to earn trust and relevance simultaneously? I run growth at CYLNDR. Worth 15 minutes at Sport Beach?

brief.who: Laura Dyer is SVP Global Marketing at SharkNinja, the consumer appliance challenger brand known for the Shark vacuum and Ninja blender/kitchen appliance lines. SharkNinja has grown aggressively through bold product innovation and high-velocity marketing, reaching ~$4.3B in revenue while maintaining the creative energy of a challenger brand. Dyer speaks at Brands&Culture Villa on Monday-Tuesday Jun 22-23. She is one of three SharkNinja contacts at Cannes 2026, alongside Dave Kersey (Global Head of Media) and Michelle Crossan-Matos (Chief Growth Officer).
brief.why: SVP Global Marketing at SharkNinja is an ICP-fit title at a brand with genuine challenger creative identity and production velocity. SharkNinja makes products people love and markets them with urgency — the brief cycle is fast, the volume is high, and the brand rewards partners who can match the pace. Three SharkNinja executives at Cannes creates a company-level concentration opportunity worth pursuing as a single account target.
hooks: SharkNinja's marketing model is built on product launches that move fast — the creative production challenge is matching product innovation cadence with brand-quality output.
At ~$4.3B revenue they're past the scrappy phase but still operate with challenger urgency. Partners who bring holding-company depth with startup pace are the sweet spot.
Three SharkNinja contacts at Cannes suggests the company is investing in new relationships. It's an open door.

drafts.warm: Hi Laura, I'll be at Brands&Culture Villa this week. SharkNinja is one of the more interesting marketing stories in consumer goods right now — challenger energy at serious scale. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. We work with brands that need production partners who move at product-launch pace. Coffee at the Villa?
drafts.direct: Laura, saw you're at Brands&Culture Villa this week. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. We work with consumer goods brands on high-velocity creative production. 15 minutes at the Villa?
drafts.curious: Laura, SharkNinja's launch cadence is one of the most demanding in consumer goods — new products every few months, all needing creative that reflects the brand's energy. What breaks first on the production side when the pipeline gets that full? I run growth at CYLNDR. Worth 15 minutes at Cannes?

brief.who: Dave Kersey is Global Head of Media at SharkNinja, speaking at Brand Innovators Beach Stage on Tuesday Jun 23 at 12:25 PM in a panel on 'From Reach to Relevance.' He is the media-side counterpart to Laura Dyer (SVP Marketing) and Michelle Crossan-Matos (CGO) at SharkNinja — one of three confirmed company contacts at Cannes 2026. SharkNinja is a ~$4.3B consumer appliance challenger brand with aggressive media investment and fast creative cycles.
brief.why: Global Head of Media at SharkNinja is an EXPANDED_TITLES ICP match. While media-side, Kersey is the person who evaluates whether production output meets media performance standards — which makes him a relevant partner entry point even if the final creative buy decision sits with Dyer. The three-contact SharkNinja picture creates a concentrated access opportunity: Dyer for the production relationship, Kersey for media validation, Crossan-Matos for strategic context.
hooks: 'From Reach to Relevance' is the media performance frame — he's thinking about whether creative earns attention, not just fills placements. CYLNDR's pitch starts with creative that performs.
Global Head of Media is the person who sees which creative formats are working and which aren't — he's downstream of production decisions but upstream of media budgets.
Pairing outreach to Kersey and Dyer in the same company creates a warm intro opportunity: meeting one reinforces the other.

drafts.warm: Hi Dave, I'll be at Brand Innovators Tuesday for 'From Reach to Relevance.' SharkNinja's media model at this scale — fast launches, high volume, challenger energy — is one of the harder production coordination challenges in consumer goods. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. Coffee after your panel?
drafts.direct: Dave, saw you're speaking Tuesday at Brand Innovators. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. We work with consumer goods brands on the creative-to-media performance loop. 15 minutes Tuesday afternoon?
drafts.curious: Dave, the reach-to-relevance question at SharkNinja's launch velocity must be sharp — lots of creative, lots of channels, high performance bar. What's the production bottleneck that actually costs media performance? I run growth at CYLNDR. Worth 15 minutes at Brand Innovators?

sadira-furlow-tonys-chocolonely.name: Sadira Furlow
sadira-furlow-tonys-chocolonely.title: Chief of Global Brand & Communications Officer

brief.who: Sadira Furlow is Chief of Global Brand & Communications Officer at Tony's Chocolonely, the Dutch DTC purpose-driven chocolate challenger brand known for its mission against modern slavery in the cocoa supply chain. Tony's is a fast-growing private brand with strong DTC distribution, retail presence, and one of the most distinctive brand identities in food/bev — bold colors, direct storytelling, mission-forward but not preachy. She speaks at LBB & Friends Beach, CYLNDR's highest-priority Cannes venue for prospect density.
brief.why: Tony's Chocolonely is flagged as CYLNDR's highest-priority new fit entry for this cycle. Furlow is the CBO-equivalent — she owns brand strategy, creative direction, and communications globally. The LBB Beach venue is ideal: the right creative industry crowd, relaxed format, and high density of brand-side decision-makers. Purpose-driven DTC challenger brand at growth stage, mission-led storytelling requirements, and international scale all point directly at CYLNDR's production and creative model.
hooks: Tony's mission story is the product — the creative challenge is keeping it fresh, urgent, and not tiresome when the brand runs the same theme year after year across global markets.
LBB Beach is a creative industry venue, which means Furlow is here to have real conversations, not pitch or be pitched. Lead with curiosity about the creative challenge.
Global brand, multiple markets, mission-led production — the operational complexity is high. Partners who can operate across geographies without brand drift are rare.

drafts.warm: Hi Sadira, I'll be at LBB Beach this week. Tony's is one of the few brands where the mission IS the creative challenge — keeping the supply chain story urgent and interesting across every format and market is harder than it looks. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. Coffee at LBB Beach?
drafts.direct: Sadira, I'll be at LBB Beach. I run growth at CYLNDR, Cheil's creative and production studio. We work with purpose-driven DTC brands on the mission-to-creative translation challenge. 15 minutes?
drafts.curious: Sadira, Tony's has one of the most singular brand voices in food/bev — the mission makes every campaign load-bearing. How do you keep that story fresh and urgent when it's also the foundation? I run growth at CYLNDR. Worth 15 minutes at LBB Beach?
